inputs.research_topic: Alzheimer's Disease Neuroinflammation and Microglial Dysfunction
inputs.keywords: APOE4, TREM2, microglial activation, neuroinflammation, amyloid-beta, tau pathology, blood-brain barrier, complement system, inflammatory cytokines, IL-1β, TNF-α, synaptic pruning
inputs.additional_information: Research scope: Focus on microglial-driven neuroinflammation in Alzheimer's disease (AD), particularly the role of genetic risk factors (APOE4, TREM2 variants) in modulating microglial phenotype and function. Include analysis of: (1) Microglial activation states (homeostatic vs disease-associated microglia), (2) Amyloid-beta and tau interaction with innate immune responses, (3) Blood-brain barrier dysfunction and peripheral immune cell infiltration, (4) Complement cascade activation and synaptic loss, (5) Age-related changes in microglial function. Integrate pathway analysis for neuroinflammatory signaling (NF-κB, NLRP3 inflammasome), protein-protein interactions between AD risk genes and immune regulators, and gene expression patterns from single-cell RNA-seq studies. Generate 2-3 testable mechanistic hypotheses linking microglial dysfunction to cognitive decline.
